rules.disabledBehavior: Removes the dylib launcher wrapper once no runtime patch still needs it, restoring the original Telegram executable launch.
rules.riskNote: The wrapper only sets DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES for Telegram; it does not modify Telegram's own bytes. The dylib's hooks stay inert until a feature patch enables them.
rules.summary: Injects Patchgram.dylib into Telegram Desktop through a DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES launcher wrapper. This is the base hook every runtime patch loads through; enabling it on its own loads the dylib with no behavior change so the runtime hooks are present.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Dylib injection installs a DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES launcher wrapper and is independent of the Telegram Desktop build version.
rules.title: Dylib injection

rules.disabledBehavior: Removes the overlay button/panel from Telegram.
rules.riskNote: Cosmetic local overlay drawn by the injected dylib via AppKit; it does not modify Telegram's own bytes or data.
rules.summary: Shows a native AppKit overlay inside Telegram: a floating button opens a panel where you pick a .png OR an animated .tgs sticker plus an animation style; with it on, the chosen image rains over an open profile and auto-follows it. Drawn by Patchgram.dylib — no Telegram bytes are patched.
rules.supportedBuildNote: The overlay is a native AppKit window, independent of the Telegram Desktop build version.
rules.title: Profile rain overlay

rules.disabledBehavior: Stops writing MTProto logs (existing log files are kept). Takes effect on the next Telegram launch.
rules.riskNote: Diagnostic only: the dylib observes the request/response buffers it already hooks and appends them to local .log files; it does not modify Telegram's bytes or traffic. The logs can contain sensitive request/response data, so treat the files as private.
rules.summary: Logs every MTProto request Telegram sends and every response it receives, each with a timestamp, to log_<start>.log files inside Telegram.app/Contents/Resources/logs_mtproto_pg. The logger opens its file in the injected dylib's constructor — before Telegram sends its first packet — so the trace is complete from launch. Each line records the direction, request id, TL constructor, and a hex preview of the body. Drawn by Patchgram.dylib — no Telegram bytes are patched.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Uses the dylib's existing send/receive hooks; independent of byte-level signatures, so it is resilient across Telegram Desktop builds.
rules.title: MTProto request/response logger

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores the original load-and-branch instructions from the executable backup pattern.
rules.riskNote: This is version-sensitive binary patching; Patchgram refuses to patch when the expected instruction windows are not unique.
rules.summary: Patches the serialized account.updateStatus request so its offline Bool is always boolTrue.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Always offline

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original message activity, read-history, draft-sync, scheduled-send, and Fact Check behavior.
rules.riskNote: These are client-side serialization patches. Telegram can still update local state; this prevents the patched requests from being serialized with valid MTProto method ids.
rules.summary: Groups message privacy tweaks: block typing activity, block read receipts, keep drafts local, schedule sends locally, and locally inject custom Fact Check blocks.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Message settings

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original hidden-url confirmation branch.
rules.riskNote: This is a local UI patch. It bypasses Telegram's external-link confirmation dialog, including suspicious or mismatched display links.
rules.summary: Skips Telegram's hidden-url confirmation branch so links open directly without the Open this link? warning.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Open links without warning

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original add-contact flags, including the add_phone_privacy_exception bit when Telegram sets it.
rules.riskNote: This is a client-side request serialization patch. It only affects the add-contact request path matched by the built-in ARM64 pattern.
rules.summary: Forces the serialized contacts.addContact flags to keep only f_note, preventing add_phone_privacy_exception from being sent.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Don't share phone when adding contacts

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores the original 6-account add/menu/storage checks.
rules.riskNote: This only changes local client-side limits. Telegram server account/session rules still apply.
rules.summary: Raises Telegram's local account add/menu/storage checks from the upstream 6-account cap to 999 accounts.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: 999 accounts

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original URL/Auth-only inline button hover behavior.
rules.riskNote: This is a local UI patch only. It shows data already present in the message markup and does not send bot callbacks by itself.
rules.summary: Treats bot callback, WebView, and SimpleWebView inline buttons as URL-like for hover/copy text so callback-data is visible locally.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Show bot callback-data on hover

rules.disabledBehavior: Stops the runtime memory overrides and keeps the original on-disk Telegram executable bytes restored.
rules.riskNote: This is a local runtime patch. It scans known arm64 byte windows on startup/config reload instead of rewriting them on disk, so exact semantic coverage can still differ between Telegram Desktop versions until each path is replaced by a higher-level hook.
rules.summary: Uses Patchgram.dylib to disable selected monetization config, gift/status requests, paid reactions, Premium effects, and local Premium/Stars/Gifts/Boost UI gates at runtime.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Disable Premium, Stars, TON & Gifts

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original local Premium availability stream.
rules.riskNote: This is a local UI patch only. Server-side Premium entitlements, payments, boosts, and account state are unchanged.
rules.summary: Forces the current session's local Premium availability stream to report true for UI gates, following 's localPremium behavior.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Local Telegram Premium

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original MTPstarsTonAmount to CreditsAmount conversion.

parameter.prompt: Enter the TON amount to show in My TON and transaction status responses.
parameter.title: TON value

rules.riskNote: This is still a local display patch; it rewrites the decoded balance amount in the built app and does not change server-side TON data.
rules.summary: Overrides Telegram's local conversion of TON balances from payments.StarsStatus responses, including My TON and payments.getStarsTransactions results.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Custom TON value

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original MTPstarsAmount to CreditsAmount conversion.

parameter.prompt: Enter the Stars amount to show in My Stars and transaction status responses.
parameter.title: Stars value

rules.riskNote: This is still a local display patch; it rewrites the decoded balance amount in the built app and does not change server-side Stars data.
rules.summary: Overrides Telegram's local conversion of Stars balances from payments.StarsStatus responses, including My Stars and payments.getStarsTransactions results.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Custom Stars value

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original sensitive media blur behavior.
rules.riskNote: This is a local display behavior change. It does not change Telegram server-side content metadata.
rules.summary: Installs a runtime memory patch for local sensitive-content blur suppression, forcing media sensitivity checks to return false locally.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Sensitive blur

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's default limit of 20 recent stickers in the panel.
rules.riskNote: Local display-only change to the in-panel recent-sticker cap; it does not change server data. The number actually shown is still bounded by how many recent stickers exist (server limit ~200).
rules.summary: Raises the recent stickers display limit from Telegram's default 20 to 200, so the "Recent" row in the sticker panel shows many more recently-used stickers. Runtime memory patch on the kRecentDisplayLimit comparison in StickersListWidget::collectRecentStickers.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Derived from Telegram Desktop 6.9.3 (arm64). Other builds are patched only when the exact byte pattern matches.
rules.title: More recent stickers

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original user/channel verification-status flags.

parameter.prompt: Choose the local peer badge and target mode to force.
parameter.title: Peer badge

rules.riskNote: This rewrites local client peer flags only. Server-side account state and Telegram trust labels are unchanged.
rules.summary: Forces a local verification-status flag on peers. Choose Verified, Scam, or Fake, then apply it to all peers, all except your own user, or only your own user.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Visual peer badge

rules.disabledBehavior: Removes the Patchgram runtime hook and restores Telegram's original executable entry point.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side runtime patch. It does not change Telegram server-side verification state.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook for Telegram's bot verification details. Choose who receives the local bot verification and which preset to use.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Bot verification

rules.disabledBehavior: Removes the Patchgram runtime hook and restores Telegram's original executable entry point when no other runtime patches are enabled.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not change Telegram server-side Stars rating data.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook for Telegram's user Stars rating. Choose who receives the local rating and set level, rating, current_level_rating, and next_level_rating.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Custom level rating

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original profile phone value handling.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not change your Telegram account phone number or privacy settings.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that returns an empty profile phone value for your own user, causing Telegram Desktop to omit the mobile phone row in the self-profile.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Hide self phone

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original self phone and user id display values.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not change your Telegram account phone, real PeerId, authorization, or server state.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that overrides the phone string stored on your own UserData and provides a local display-only user id for Patchgram visual details.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Self identity override

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original personal channel value.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not attach a channel to your Telegram account or change server-side profile data.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that writes a display-only personal channel id into your own UserData so Telegram Desktop shows it as attached in the self-profile.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Local attached channel

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original Fragment phone collectible detection and response data.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not mint or transfer a Fragment collectible phone number.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that makes selected phone rows look collectible and stores local fragment.collectibleInfo values for the phone collectible dialog.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Fragment phone

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original star gifts and their data.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch on the gift list response. It does not move, mint or change any gift server-side; only what your client displays changes.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that rewrites the saved star gifts in the payments.savedStarGifts response before Telegram reads it, so a profile's gifts show the sender, date, gift id and Stars price you configure. Each value is optional (0 = leave the original).
rules.supportedBuildNote: Hooks the MTProto gift-list response and rewrites it with the in-dylib TL walker, so it is independent of byte-level signatures across Telegram builds.
rules.title: Spoof profile gifts

rules.disabledBehavior: Shows only the gifts Telegram returns.
rules.riskNote: Local client-side display patch on the available-gifts response. The extra gifts are shown locally; buying a gift that the server doesn't actually offer will still fail server-side.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that appends extra star gifts (price 50 Stars, not limited) to the payments.starGifts response, so they appear in the gift purchase menu. Their stickers use the matching custom emoji (resolved from api.changes.tg).
rules.supportedBuildNote: Hooks the MTProto available-gifts response and appends entries with the in-dylib TL walker, independent of byte-level signatures.
rules.title: Show hidden gifts

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original self-profile usernames and Fragment username collectible response data.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side display patch. It does not reserve, mint, buy, or assign Telegram usernames on the server.
rules.summary: Installs a local runtime hook that replaces the usernames list shown for your self-profile and returns local Fragment collectible info for configured collectible usernames.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Custom list usernames

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original premium sticker effect start behavior.
rules.riskNote: This is a local visual patch. It does not change sticker, Premium, or account state on Telegram servers.
rules.summary: Installs a runtime memory patch that returns from Sticker::checkPremiumEffectStart before Telegram can start premium sticker effects.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Disable premium effects

rules.disabledBehavior: Keeps Telegram's original media spoiler behavior.
rules.riskNote: This is a local client-side media display patch. It does not change spoiler metadata already stored on Telegram servers.
rules.summary: Installs runtime memory patches that suppress local media spoiler flags and rendering, so spoiler-marked photos and videos are shown normally.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Disable media spoilers

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram's original stories state handling and request constructor ids.
rules.riskNote: This blocks the known local receive/fetch paths for stories in the selected ARM64 build. Telegram may add new story request paths in future builds.
rules.summary: Clears local stories state on users/channels and invalidates the known story fetch/read/view request constructors.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Hide stories

rules.disabledBehavior: Restores Telegram Ads and proxy sponsor promotion behavior.
rules.riskNote: Telegram Ads are blocked through the sponsored-message request path. Proxy sponsor is handled as a separate top-promotion surface and may need a new byte window if Telegram changes its promo flow.
rules.summary: Disables Telegram Ads and proxy sponsor promotion surfaces.
rules.supportedBuildNote: Built-in ARM64 patterns were derived from Telegram Desktop 6.8.4 and 6.8.5. Other builds are patched only when the exact byte patterns match.
rules.title: Disable ads
